name: Peches Aux Epices
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 12
Spread a small square of cheesecloth on a tray. Place the cloves, gingerroot, cinnamon stick, and allspice in the center, then tie shut with string.
Slice the orange thinly.
Place peaches in a large pot of boiling water for 10 seconds. Remove with a slotted spoon and plunge into a bowl of ice water. Peel and set aside.
Repeat the blanching and chilling process with the orange slices and set aside.
In a medium-heavy saucepan over medium heat, combine sugar and vinegar. Heat until the sugar is dissolved.
Add the spice bag and peaches to the pan. Cook over low heat for 8 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and divide between 2 or 3 two-cup glass preserving jars.
Add orange slices to the same sugar mixture. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes, then remove with a slotted spoon and add equal amounts to each of the jars of peaches.
Discard the spice bag. Boil the syrup over medium heat for 4 - 5 minutes.
Pour over the fruit in the jars. Let cool, then seal shut. Store in a cool place for up to 1 month, then refrigerate. Serve with roast pork, goose, or ham. Makes from 2 to 3 pints.
